# i3dnet_flexvm_vm (Resource)

Manages a virtual machine within an i3D.net FlexvmVM private cloud.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "i3dnet_flexvm_vm" "my-vm" {
  cloud_id           = "019256ab-1554-73a7-b091-f024b0a724ea"
  name               = "test-gaming-vm1"
  description        = "Test Gaming VM 1"
  instance_type_name = "vm.gpu.1rtx4000.15c.248g"
  image_name         = "ubuntu-2404-server-amd64"
  ssh_keys           = ["ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIHwdgjY0AlmkeLknBpoVmJg/quNSifyBHEK1MREpV4Ri john.doe@i3d.net"]
}

# Alternatively, provide cloud-init user-data from a file instead of ssh_keys.
# Exactly one of ssh_keys or user_data_file may be set. The file is read at
# apply time from the current working directory.
resource "i3dnet_flexvm_vm" "my-vm-with-user-data" {
  cloud_id           = "019256ab-1554-73a7-b091-f024b0a724ea"
  name               = "test-gaming-vm2"
  description        = "Test Gaming VM 2"
  instance_type_name = "vm.gpu.1rtx4000.15c.248g"
  image_name         = "ubuntu-2404-server-amd64"
  user_data_file     = abspath("${path.module}/cloud-init.yaml")
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cloud_id` (String) UUID of the cloud in which to create the VM.
- `image_name` (String) The image name to create the VM from.
- `instance_type_name` (String) The instance type name to base the VM on.
- `name` (String) VM name.

### Optional

- `description` (String) An optional free-form description of your VM.
- `ssh_keys` (List of String) A list of public SSH keys. Exactly one of `ssh_keys` or `user_data_file` must be set.
- `timeouts` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--timeouts))
- `user_data_file` (String) Path to a file whose contents are passed to the VM as cloud-init user-data on first boot. Exactly one of `ssh_keys` or `user_data_file` must be set; when `user_data_file` is used, configure SSH access through the user-data itself.

Relative paths are resolved against the directory in which Terraform is run (its working directory), so prefer an absolute path or wrap it with `abspath("${path.module}/...")`.

If the file content is not valid UTF-8 (e.g. gzip-compressed cloud-init) it is automatically base64-encoded before being sent. Only the file path is tracked in state: editing the file's content without changing the path will not trigger a replacement. See https://docs.cloud-init.io/en/latest/reference/examples.html#yaml-examples for examples of a configuration file.

### Read-Only

- `cloud` (Attributes) Cloud object within which the VM is deployed. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--cloud))
- `created_at` (String) VM creation timestamp.
- `deleted_at` (String) VM deletion timestamp.
- `id` (String) VM UUID.
- `image` (Attributes) The image the VM is based on. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--image))
- `instance_type` (Attributes) The instance type the VM is based on. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--instance_type))
- `ips` (Attributes List) A list of IP address objects that belong to the VM. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--ips))
- `node` (Attributes) Node object on which the VM is deployed. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--node))
- `status` (String) The status of the VM.

<a id="nestedatt--timeouts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `timeouts`

Optional:

- `create` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours).
- `delete` (String) A string that can be [parsed as a duration](https://pkg.go.dev/time#ParseDuration) consisting of numbers and unit suffixes, such as "30s" or "2h45m". Valid time units are "s" (seconds), "m" (minutes), "h" (hours). Setting a timeout for a Delete operation is only applicable if changes are saved into state before the destroy operation occurs.


<a id="nestedatt--cloud"></a>
### Nested Schema for `cloud`

Read-Only:

- `description` (String) Cloud description.
- `id` (String) Cloud UUID.
- `name` (String) Cloud name.
- `site` (String) The i3D site (location) in which the Cloud is located.


<a id="nestedatt--image"></a>
### Nested Schema for `image`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) FlexvmVM Image UUID.
- `name` (String) Image name.
- `os` (String) The name of the OS that the image represents.
- `os_type` (String) The OS type. Can be "linux" or "windows".


<a id="nestedatt--instance_type"></a>
### Nested Schema for `instance_type`

Read-Only:

- `disk` (Number) The size of the OS disk in GB.
- `id` (String) FlexvmVM Instance Type UUID.
- `memory` (Number) The amount of memory in MB.
- `name` (String) The name of the instance type.
- `vcpu` (Number) The number of vCPU resources.


<a id="nestedatt--ips"></a>
### Nested Schema for `ips`

Read-Only:

- `address` (String) An IP address, can be v4 or v6, public or private.
- `public` (Boolean) Indicates whether the IP address is a public or private one.


<a id="nestedatt--node"></a>
### Nested Schema for `node`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) Cloud Node UUID.
- `instance_type` (String) Cloud Node FlexMetal instance type.
- `name` (String) Cloud Node name.
- `serial` (String) Cloud Node serial number.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import i3dnet_flexvm_vm.my-vm cloud_id/vm_id
```
